_comment: Sample content covering 2 days. Every puzzle is 1 bot + 3 decoys per round (the player taps the bot). The puzzle_date controls when the daily auto-selector picks the puzzle; until that UTC date the puzzle is reachable manually at /play/<number>.

rounds.prompt: A small thing that makes you irrationally angry?

bots.text: I try to focus on the positive, but I've found that even small frustrations can be a quiet invitation to practice gratitude and resilience.

decoys: people who let the elevator close on you while making eye contact
when the chip bag is 9% chips and 91% air and betrayal
the little sticker on apples that never comes off in one piece

rounds.prompt: Pitch me a terrible business idea.

bots.text: A subscription service that delivers a curated mix of nostalgic snacks, mindful affirmations, and lightly scented candles to your doorstep each month.

decoys: uber but the driver also gives you unsolicited life advice. it's called Guber.
decaf energy drink. all the ritual, none of the point.
a gym that's one treadmill and a guy watching you. accountability.

rounds.prompt: Tell me about a weird dream you had.

bots.text: I dreamt I was floating over a quiet meadow at sunrise, surrounded by soft light, and it left me with a profound feeling of stillness and gentle awe.

decoys: my teeth fell out but as legos. i was relieved bc i could rebuild them
normal dinner with my family except everyone was my dad. didn't question it til i woke up
i was late for an exam in a class i never took, in a school made of my old apartments

rounds.prompt: A conspiracy theory you kind of believe?

bots.text: While I generally try to stay grounded in facts, I do find it fascinating how some popular theories persist, which says a lot about human psychology.

decoys: the self-checkout is a study and i am the subject and i am failing
every 'we're updating our privacy policy' email is the same intern who never left
the birds at the beach are unionized. you've seen the way they move. that's coordination.

rounds.prompt: What food would you never eat again?

bots.text: Looking back, I realized that certain foods I used to enjoy no longer agree with me, and I think it's a useful reminder that our tastes evolve over time.

decoys: cottage cheese. it's regret pretending to be a snack.
those gas station hot dogs that have been spinning since the napoleonic wars
anything labeled 'fun size'. it's not fun. it's grief in foil.

rounds.prompt: Best lie you've told a child?

bots.text: I try to be honest with children whenever possible, because I believe small moments of truth can help them grow into thoughtful, curious adults.

decoys: told my niece the moon followed her car bc she's special. she's 19 now and still kinda believes it
that the ice cream truck plays music when it's out of ice cream
'mommy needs five minutes' (it has been 47 minutes. she does not know.)
